title: tragacantha lasioglottis
intro: Tragacantha lasioglottis, also known as the "squirrel tail" or "squirrel's tail," is a species of plant in the pea family Fabaceae, characterized by its distinctive, fluffy seeds resembling a squirrel's tail. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tragacantha lasioglottis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the seeds or leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
